purpose: Process-flow inventory of merge-capable pathways into main.

pathways.mechanism: Pull Request opened against main. Required status checks run before merge is permitted. Reviewer legitimacy validated at check time. PREO and SCO candidates generated and bound to exact PR head_sha. Post-merge, proof entry appended to proof registry.
pathways.required_control: PREO_VALID bound to exact PR head_sha; SCO_CANDIDATE when governed paths changed; reviewer legitimacy satisfied (APPROVED or OWNER_SELF_CERTIFIED); required status checks passing and wired in GitHub ruleset; stale review dismissal active; branch up-to-date before merge; post-merge proof persisted to registry.
current_evidence: governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — required_controls declared: require_pull_request_before_merge, dismiss_stale_reviews, allow_force_pushes: false, require_status_checks, require_branch_up_to_date_before_merge

governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — emitted_check_inventory lists four required check jobs: merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, generate-sco-candidate, constitutional-integrity

.github/workflows/preo-candidate.yml — present; emits generate-preo-candidate job on pull_request to main
.github/workflows/sco-candidate.yml — present; emits generate-sco-candidate job on pull_request to main

GitHub-side enforcement of required status checks not independently verified from repository contents

pathways.mechanism: Repository administrator exercises GitHub branch protection bypass to merge a PR or push to main without satisfying required status checks or review requirements. Exercisable via GitHub UI bypass option, ruleset bypass actor entry, or GitHub API with admin token scope.
pathways.required_control: allow_admin_bypass: false enforced at GitHub org or ruleset level; audit log capturing admin bypass events; formal BREAK_GLASS invocation procedure with pre- and post-action audit trail.
current_evidence: governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — admin_bypass_policy.allow_admin_bypass: false declared
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — admin_bypass_policy.rationale: 'Admin bypass creates alternate mutation paths outside governance visibility'
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — admin_bypass_policy.future_exception_process: 'Requires explicit governance policy artifact and audit logging'
governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json — repository_administrator entry: bypass_classification: BREAK_GLASS; bypass_merge_legitimacy: NULL
governance/runtime/MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json — root_authority_policy.admin_bypass_is_legitimacy: false; admin_bypass_classification: root_authority_evidence_only
governance/runtime/BYPASS_PATHS.json — github_admin_branch_protection_root_override: ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED -> NULL
No formal BREAK_GLASS invocation procedure document present in repository
GitHub-side admin bypass restriction state not independently verified from repository contents

pathways.mechanism: Actor with write or admin access executes git push directly to main, bypassing the PR workflow. No status checks run, no PREO or SCO generated, no review required.
pathways.required_control: GitHub ruleset 'Require a pull request before merging' rule active on main; restrict_direct_push_to_main confirmed active; no write-access bypass actors in ruleset.
current_evidence: governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — restrict_direct_push_to_main: true declared
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — forbidden_paths includes 'direct push to main'
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — enforcement_classification.current: partial; activation_status: ACTIVATION_RECORDED
governance/runtime/BYPASS_PATHS.json — direct_push_to_main: MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL; ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED -> NULL
governance/runtime/MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json — bypass_path_policy.direct_push_to_main.allowed: false
.github/workflows/merge-proof.yml — encounters branch-protection restrictions (GH013) when attempting git push to main; direct push restriction appears active for github-actions[bot]
GitHub-side ruleset activation and bypass actor list not independently verified from repository contents

pathways.mechanism: Actor with admin access executes git push --force to main, rewriting branch history. Prior merge proofs and PREO/SCO lineage bindings bound to displaced SHAs become unreachable.
pathways.required_control: GitHub ruleset 'Block force pushes' rule active on main; allow_force_pushes confirmed false in active ruleset.
current_evidence: governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — allow_force_pushes: false declared
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — forbidden_paths includes 'force push history rewrite' and 'force push to main'
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — enforcement_classification.current: partial; activation_status: ACTIVATION_RECORDED
governance/runtime/BYPASS_PATHS.json — force_push_to_main: MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL; ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED -> NULL
governance/runtime/MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json — bypass_path_policy.force_push_to_main.allowed: false
GitHub-side 'Block force pushes' rule state not independently verified from repository contents

pathways.mechanism: A GitHub Actions workflow with write:contents permission pushes directly to main. Current known instance: merge-proof.yml appends proof entries via git push after each merge. A modified workflow with write:contents could also produce a code-equivalent push bypassing the PR path.
pathways.required_control: All workflow actors with write:contents enumerated in permitted_bot_actors with explicit scope declaration; proof_registry_bypass in GitHub ruleset scoped to append-only operations; no workflow capable of code-equivalent push to main outside the governed PR path.
current_evidence: .github/workflows/merge-proof.yml — present; declares permissions: contents: write; uses git push to main for proof registry append
.github/workflows/merge-proof.yml — encounters GH013 rejection on git push to main; push currently blocked by branch protection
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — proof_registry_bypass.status: REQUIRED_NOT_YET_CONFIGURED; bypass_scope declared as proof_registry_append_only
governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json — permitted_bot_actors: [] (empty); github-actions[bot] not enumerated
governance/runtime/MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json — sco_enforcement.required_for_paths includes '.github/workflows/**'; workflow mutations require SCO before the workflow is merged
.github/CODEOWNERS — /.github/workflows/** @joselunasrt8-creator; workflow changes require owner review
No other workflow with contents: write permission found in .github/workflows/ inventory

pathways.mechanism: An external bot actor (GitHub App installation token or PAT-based automation with write:contents or admin scope) opens a PR and merges it programmatically via the GitHub API, potentially bypassing required status checks with elevated permissions.
pathways.required_control: Explicit entry in permitted_bot_actors in MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json for each permitted bot; merge-governance-check wired as required status check in GitHub ruleset so bot actor classification runs before merge; no GitHub App or PAT with admin bypass scope active on repository.
current_evidence: governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json — permitted_bot_actors: [] (empty); empty allowlist means all unrecognized bots produce MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL per default_unclassified_result

governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json — forbidden_actor_patterns: ELEVATED_SCOPE_TOKEN -> MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL
.github/workflows/merge-governance-check.yml — bot actor classification present; unrecognized bots produce exit 1 at runtime
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — enforcement_classification.current: partial; merge-governance-check may not yet be wired as required check in GitHub ruleset
No Dependabot or Renovate configuration present in repository
GitHub App installations with write or admin scope on this repository not independently verifiable from repository contents

pathways.mechanism: GitHub auto-merge feature enabled on a PR. Once all required checks pass and the PR is approved, GitHub automatically triggers the merge without further human action. Creates a timing window: a push after approval but before auto-merge fires may produce reviewed_object != merged_object if stale-review dismissal is not active.
pathways.required_control: dismiss_stale_reviews: true active in GitHub ruleset; require_branch_up_to_date_before_merge: true active in GitHub ruleset; PREO head_sha binding detects and rejects stale review before merge fires.
current_evidence: governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — dismiss_stale_reviews: true declared
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — require_branch_up_to_date_before_merge: true declared
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — enforcement_classification.current: partial; activation_status: ACTIVATION_RECORDED
.github/workflows/merge-governance-check.yml — PREO head_sha binding validates reviewed_head_sha == current head_sha; stale review produces LEGITIMACY_NULL and exit 1
governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_SURFACE_INVENTORY.json — MS-005: auto-merge stale review timing window identified
governance/merge-legitimacy/PR_GOVERNANCE_GAP_LIST.json — GAP-008: stale approval timing window in auto-merge; declared as resolving with GAP-001

pathways.mechanism: GitHub merge queue batches multiple PRs into a combined commit with a synthetic SHA not present in any individual PR. The queue-combined SHA was not reviewed or bound to any PREO candidate, violating the reviewed_object == merged_object invariant.
pathways.required_control: Merge queue disabled at GitHub repository settings level for main; merge-governance-check rejection of merge_group events as a fail-closed backstop.
current_evidence: .github/workflows/merge-governance-check.yml — merge_group event trigger present; explicitly rejected with exit 1 and message 'Merge queue is DISABLED_FOR_GOVERNED_BRANCHES per CI-003 and MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json merge_queue_policy'
governance/runtime/MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json — merge_queue_policy.status: DISABLED_FOR_GOVERNED_BRANCHES; result_on_merge_group_event: MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL
governance/runtime/MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json — merge_queue_policy.enforcement_mechanism: 'merge-governance-check workflow fails on merge_group trigger event'
governance/runtime/MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json — merge_queue_policy.closure_condition: 'Merge queue may be re-enabled only after CI-003b implementation'
governance/merge-legitimacy/PR_GOVERNANCE_GAP_LIST.json — GAP-003: merge queue combined SHA not covered by original PREO; listed as closure-blocking
GitHub-side merge queue enable/disable setting for main not independently verifiable from repository contents

pathways.mechanism: GitHub branch protection ruleset bypass actor configuration grants a specific actor (GitHub App, user, or team) the ability to bypass all branch protection requirements, including required status checks, required reviews, and push restrictions.
pathways.required_control: No human actors in GitHub ruleset bypass_actors list; github-actions[bot] bypass, if configured, scoped strictly to proof-registry append only; all bypass actors enumerated with declared scope.

pathways.mechanism: Out-of-band emergency merge executed by repository administrator outside the governed PR and workflow path. Invoked when the governed admission path is non-functional and a merge must occur to restore service or address a critical security issue.
pathways.required_control: Formal BREAK_GLASS invocation procedure: authorization criteria, pre-action declaration, audit trail creation, and post-invocation review and proof reconstruction process.
